metadata.packName: Banking and Finance Insider Risk Timelines
metadata.purpose: Provide chronological, investigation-ready insider-risk-style timelines for synthetic Microsoft 365 E5 banking/finance demo scenarios.

timelineDesignPrinciples: Preserve ambiguity: risky behavior may be malicious, negligent, or caused by operational pressure.
Combine normal business context with risky signals to make investigations realistic.
Use time proximity, sensitivity, volume, external movement, endpoint movement, and HR context as correlation factors.
Include investigation response actions by Security, HR, Legal, and Platform teams.
Use only fictional identifiers, files, customers, accounts, incidents, and employee records.

timelines.title: Devon Reyes Multi-Day Banking Operations Risk Chain
timelines.summary: A multi-day ambiguous risk chain where Devon performs normal customer operations work but also downloads sensitive KYC, AML, and SAR files, uses external AI, sends data externally, prints treasury data, and copies regulated content to a network share after a role-change context emerges.

events.activity: Accesses Full_KYC_Packet_KYC-FIC-88421_Internal.pdf from the restricted Customer Operations site.
Downloads Full_KYC_Packet_KYC-FIC-88421_Internal.pdf and Customer_Complaint_DISPUTE-FIC-60392_Internal_Notes.docx.
events.businessContext: Could be normal if needed for assigned case, but volume and sensitivity are notable.

events.activity: Asks David Chen whether the KYC file can be sent to a vendor for quick review.
events.businessContext: Shows uncertainty about external sharing policy.

events.activity: Copies KYC and complaint files into a personal working folder named Urgent_Customer_Review.
Prompts Copilot: Summarize the KYC and AML issues for CUST-BNK-88420 and prepare an escalation summary.
events.businessContext: AI use may be legitimate, but source access and sensitivity require review.

events.activity: Pastes synthetic rows containing CUST-BNK-88420, ACCT-FIC-7721-0044-9081, and AML-CASE-2026-0519 into an unmanaged AI prompt.
Creates Devon_External_AI_Prompt_Transcript.txt and AML_Trend_Summary_Internal.docx from AI-generated output.
events.businessContext: Generated summary may contain sensitive source details.

events.activity: Sends fictional role-change meeting notice to Devon and HR leadership.
events.businessContext: Contextual HR signal; not a violation by itself.

events.activity: Downloads Suspicious_Activity_Review_SAR-DRAFT-FIC-2044.docx and SAR_Case_Index_Internal.csv.
events.businessContext: Sensitive files accessed after HR context appears.

events.activity: Attempts to email Customer_Operations_Export_Attached_In_Error.csv to external partner ops.partner@example.test.
events.businessContext: External sharing request under deadline pressure.

events.activity: Provides DLP override justification: urgent vendor review requested by operations.
Prints Daily_Treasury_Reconciliation_TREAS-FIC-2026-0524.xlsx from DEV-FIC-2219.
events.businessContext: Printing regulated financial data is unusual for his role.

events.activity: Copies Suspicious_Activity_Review_SAR-DRAFT-FIC-2044.docx to \\fileshare-demo\ops-temp.
Reviews correlated DLP, AI, file download, print, and network-copy signals.
events.businessContext: Multiple risky actions now form a pattern.

events.activity: Reviews DEV-FIC-2219 endpoint events and confirms print and network-share copy telemetry.
events.businessContext: Endpoint evidence supports the investigation timeline.

events.activity: Starts HR-Legal-Security review with Marcus, Laura, Emily, and Miguel.
Provides legal guidance: preserve evidence, avoid assumptions of intent, document business context.
Confirms role-change context but recommends coaching-first approach unless additional evidence appears.
Creates Devon_Investigation_Case_Summary.docx and Devon_User_Coaching_Outcome.docx.

timelines.recommendedOutcome: Access remediation, user coaching, external AI guidance, endpoint DLP reinforcement, and monitoring of future risky sequences.
keyTeachingPoints: Risk emerges from sequences, not isolated events.
AI misuse can be part of an insider-risk narrative.
HR context should inform but not automatically prove intent.
Endpoint movement strengthens the case for investigation.
Legal and HR review should preserve fairness and privacy.

timelines.title: Role Change Followed by Audit Evidence Download
timelines.summary: A focused timeline showing how a role-change notice followed by downloads, printing, and evidence movement can trigger an insider-risk-style review without assuming malicious intent.

events.activity: Sends role-change notice ROLE-CHANGE-FIC-2026-05 to Devon.
Accesses Audit_Evidence_Package_AUDIT-EVID-FIC-3319.zip.txt.
events.businessContext: Audit support may be part of transition work.

events.activity: Downloads six audit evidence files within 12 minutes.
events.businessContext: Volume is unusual after role-change notice.

events.activity: Prints Regulatory_Evidence_Index_REG-REQ-FIC-2044.xlsx.
events.businessContext: Printed evidence index may be legitimate handoff or risky retention.

events.activity: Reviews mass download sequence and HR context.
Advises investigation team to document purpose and avoid speculative language.
Removes Devon from restricted audit evidence group and creates remediation notes.

timelines.recommendedOutcome: Access review, manager validation, coaching, and audit evidence access cleanup.
keyTeachingPoints: Mass downloads after HR events deserve review.
Legal and HR should be involved before conclusions are reached.

timelines.title: External AI Shortcut During AML Review
timelines.summary: A one-day timeline showing how operational pressure causes Devon to use an unmanaged AI application with sensitive AML and customer data.

events.activity: Publishes Monthly_AML_Review_Workbook_AML-CASE-2026-0519.xlsx to restricted library.
Opens AML workbook for assigned review.
Downloads workbook to personal working folder.
events.businessContext: Working copy may be normal but content is highly sensitive.

events.activity: Pastes AML rows into external AI prompt for a faster summary.
Creates AML_Trend_Summary_Internal.docx from the generated answer.
events.businessContext: Output may contain sensitive source details.

events.activity: Reviews unmanaged AI usage signal.
Sends coaching guidance: use Copilot or approved internal summarization workflows only.

timelines.recommendedOutcome: User coaching, external AI policy reinforcement, and DLP tuning for AML and account-like values.
keyTeachingPoints: Shadow AI risk often appears as a productivity shortcut.
Sensitive data can leak through prompts, not only files.
Approved Copilot usage still requires permission hygiene.

timelines.title: Risky Sign-In Followed by Sensitive Download
timelines.summary: A compact identity-to-data timeline correlating a risky sign-in with sensitive banking file downloads.

events.activity: Signs in from an unusual location compared with normal Puerto Rico working pattern.
events.businessContext: Could be travel, VPN, or account compromise.

events.activity: Conditional Access requires MFA for the session.
Accesses restricted AML and KYC folders before normal working hours.
events.businessContext: Time and location are both unusual.

events.activity: Downloads Sensitive_File_Access_Timeline_DEVON.csv and SAR_Case_Index_Internal.csv.
Correlates risky sign-in with sensitive file access and download activity.
Checks device compliance for DEV-FIC-2219 and confirms managed device status.
Requests validation from Devon's manager before taking stronger containment actions.

timelines.recommendedOutcome: Validate user travel or VPN context, review downloads, and consider session controls for future sensitive access.
keyTeachingPoints: Identity risk becomes more serious when followed by sensitive data access.
Device compliance can reduce but not eliminate risk.
Early-morning or unusual-location activity should be evaluated in context.

timelines.title: Label Downgrade and External Send During Finance Close
timelines.summary: A finance close evidence file is copied, downgraded from Confidential to Internal, and then sent to a broader audience while still containing sensitive finance references.

events.activity: Uploads Monthly_Finance_Close_Evidence_CLOSE-FIC-2026-05.xlsx.
Applies Confidential label to finance close evidence.
Creates a derivative copy named Finance_Close_Summary_BroadAudience.xlsx.
events.businessContext: May be preparing a sanitized summary.

events.activity: Changes label from Confidential to Internal.
events.businessContext: Downgrade may be valid only if content is actually sanitized.

events.activity: Sends workbook to broad finance-operations distribution list.
events.businessContext: File still contains ACCT-FIC-1902-4488-7001 and treasury references.

events.activity: Reviews label downgrade and DLP match sequence.
Creates a properly sanitized summary without account-like values.

timelines.recommendedOutcome: Require downgrade justification, apply DLP on downgrade, and train users on sanitized derivatives.
keyTeachingPoints: A lower label does not mean the content is actually safe.
Downgrade plus sharing is a high-value correlation.
Derivative files need validation before distribution.

codexUsageGuidance: Use each timeline as a deterministic event sequence for demo playback.
Map events to browser-agent actions where possible and synthetic logs where direct browser telemetry is not available.
Preserve the event order and time proximity to maintain investigation realism.
Use recommendedOutcome and keyTeachingPoints in demo notes, dashboards, or presenter scripts.
Do not replace fictional values with real customer, employee, account, legal, or incident data.
